subject: Databases
title: Implicit vs Explicit Transactions
description: A quick, practical guide to Implicit vs Explicit Transactions.

hook.text: SQL Server wraps every statement in an implicit transaction by default. Postgres uses explicit BEGIN/COMMIT.

buildup.text: Implicit transactions start automatically. Explicit transactions require you to write BEGIN.

discovery.text: In explicit mode, forgetting BEGIN means each statement auto-commits. No grouping, no rollback.

twist.text: Connection poolers like PgBouncer in transaction mode reset state between transactions. Session-level settings vanish.

climax.text: Know your database's default. Test transaction behavior before production, not after an incident.

punchline.text: Know implicit vs explicit. The default behavior varies by database.

quiz.question: What happens in explicit transaction mode if you forget to write BEGIN?
options: Each statement auto-commits individually, with no ability to roll back as a group
The database queues statements until you write BEGIN
All statements are silently rolled back
